name: Polecat
description:
Library component. The single Polecat archetype: a Claude Code session running with the polecat role. Models BOTH the work-coordination lifecycle (Absent -> Working -> Done -> Idle) AND the substrate-layer hook-receive boundary (HOOK_IN port).

v0.3 (#3587 support): adds Parked state plus PARK_IN / WAKE_IN ports for the parked-on-gate lifecycle. A Polecat parked on a gate waits in Parked until WAKE_IN delivers a Wake message. Projects that don't model parking (e.g., Lane in convoy-lands) bind PARK_IN and WAKE_IN to NeverFires stubs; the new transitions remain unreachable.

Different applications bind different subsets of ports:
  - convoy-lands: MERGE_READY/ISSUE_OBSERVE/SLING/HOOK_IN active; PARK_IN/WAKE_IN stubbed.
  - gate-3587: PARK_IN/WAKE_IN active; SLING/MERGE_READY stubbed (or unused via observe semantics).
Both paths to Working exist as before (sling-receive AND observe-driven).

SLING.description: receives a sling assignment from Feeder.

MERGE_READY.description: signals the refinery that work is complete (done.go:1170 nudgeRefinery). Tagged 'MergeReady'.

HOOK_IN.description: receives a Claude Code session lifecycle event (PreCompact, etc.).

PARK_IN.description: receives a Park signal when the polecat's work is blocked on a gate (#3587). Tagged 'Park'. Lane and other non-parking topologies bind this to NeverFires.

WAKE_IN.description: receives a Wake signal from Deacon (gt gate wake) or PrimeRecovery (the proposed self-heal fix). Tagged 'Wake'. Transitions Parked -> Working.

actors.name: Polecat
actors.owner: Polecat agent. v0.3 adds Parked state for the gate-blocked lifecycle.

triples.event: polecat advances by observing its assigned issue is hooked (observe-driven path)
hook handler completes; headless polecat returns to working
polecat parks on a gate (gate-3587 path)
polecat receives wake (gt gate wake or prime self-heal)
